patch.value:
## Context
The noun-verb consolidation (pm-n7rr, ADR pm-pbyu) keeps every legacy spelling alive as a hidden alias via the shim layer (pm-0z7n), and the v1.0 GA stability contract (pm-agou) will eventually freeze the grammar and decide which aliases can be retired. Today there is no way to know whether any agent or script still uses a given deprecated spelling — removal decisions would be guesses, and a wrong guess breaks working automations for zero benefit.

## Proposal
Instrument alias resolution with the existing local telemetry subsystem (counters only, no new infrastructure, same privacy posture — local-only, never in git):
- The alias shim increments a per-alias counter (alias -> canonical, count, last_seen) whenever a deprecated spelling is resolved.
- Surface the rollup in the diagnostics noun (ops stats / telemetry stats): aliases still in use, counts over the retention window, and aliases with zero recorded use.
- Document the removal criterion in the ADR: an alias is removable at a major boundary only after N releases with zero recorded use across reporting workspaces (exact N decided in pm-agou).

## Scope guard
Counting happens inside the shim resolution path only — canonical spellings cost nothing. No network, no new storage format: reuse the telemetry store and its existing disable env-vars.
Alias-usage counters: measure deprecated-spelling hits so grammar-freeze removals are evidence-based

Alias shim records per-alias hit counters (alias, canonical, count, last_seen) in the local telemetry store honoring existing telemetry disable env-vars; canonical spellings incur zero overhead; a diagnostics surface lists alias usage and zero-use aliases; the deprecation ADR documents the zero-use-for-N-releases removal criterion; unit test proves counter increment on alias resolution and absence on canonical invocation
